one_line: Signature gradient as identity (intentional, not template) + sharp typography + bold hero moments. Identity-as-design. Feels like Arc Browser, Granola, Tana.
inspired_by: Linear Loops (the brand site, not the app)

palette.$gradient_rule: This is the ONE profile where gradients are CORRECT — but only ONE signature gradient per project, used as identity, not template clutter. Pick a direction (orange-pink, blue-purple, green-teal, etc.) and use it consistently.

background.role: Dark base with a hint of cool. Backdrop for the gradient.

text_primary.role: Sharp white — this profile uses pure white text for max contrast against gradients.

gradient_signature.role: Orange → Pink → Purple. The PROJECT'S signature. Pick ONE gradient direction and reuse it for: hero panel bg, brand mark, primary CTA backgrounds.
gradient_signature.rule: PICK ONE alternative per project, never combine

accent_solid.role: Solid pink — sampled from the gradient. Used for inline links, small accents.

rules: The gradient is the brand. Not a generic AI-pink-purple template gradient — a PURPOSEFUL color choice that the user explicitly picked
Use the gradient SPARINGLY: hero section bg (one), primary CTA bg (one), brand mark fill (one). Not on every card
Outside the signature gradient, everything else is monochrome (dark surfaces, white text)
Solid accent (sampled from the gradient) is for small touches: inline link color, focus ring color, active-state indicator

display.family: Söhne Breit Halbfett, Inter Display, Inter, -apple-system, system-ui, sans-serif

mono_rounded.family: Söhne Mono, Berkeley Mono, JetBrains Mono, ui-monospace, monospace
mono_rounded.rule: Mono used sparingly as flourish — version tags, system notifications, beta labels.

rules: Headlines are bold — weight 700-800 with tight tracking
The voice is confident, brand-forward. Marketing copy reads more like a manifesto than features
Product UI screens in this aesthetic stay denser (24-32px)
Larger radii than linear-product or vercel-dev — this aesthetic embraces curve
Buttons: md (10px) or pill (full) — both work depending on context
Hero gradient panels: xl-2xl (24-32px) for that signature curved-corner feel
Glow effects ARE allowed here — they're part of the gradient identity
Primary CTA hover: brightness shift + slight scale (1.02)
But still: no clipart-style soft shadows on cards in the main flow

button_primary.rule: Primary CTAs use the signature gradient. This is the ONE place gradient buttons are correct.

hero_panel.rule: Hero panel uses the signature gradient as full background. White text on top. ONE per page.

version_chip.rule: 'v2.4' or 'BETA' chips in mono. A signature small flourish.

marketing_hero.structure: Top: small brand mark + nav. Below: a HUGE gradient panel (full viewport width, radius xl, 96-128px padding) with brand statement headline in white. Single gradient CTA + ghost outline.
do: Brand statement is short and bold — 6-10 words
Mono version chip near brand mark

product_dashboard.structure: Mostly dark monochrome surfaces. Gradient appears only on: the brand mark in the sidebar, the primary CTA, possibly an active-state indicator strip.
do: Restraint in product UI — gradient is for marketing/identity moments, not chrome

pricing.structure: If two tiers: the recommended tier has a thin gradient border (1-2px), others have neutral borders. If single tier: gradient CTA, monochrome rest.
do: Subtle gradient as a focusing tool, not a flashing one

animations.library: gsap with custom easings + WebGL/canvas if budget allows
animations.philosophy: Identity-driven motion. The gradient should LIVE — slow continuous loops in the background.

patterns.gradient_loop: Background-position animation on the signature gradient, 12-20s linear loop. Subtle but present.
patterns.cta_glow: Primary CTA has a soft gradient-colored glow that pulses slowly (3s loop, opacity 0.6 → 1.0).

anti_patterns: spinning gradients (the gradient should drift, not rotate fast)
